observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Chairperson of the Inventory Committee under the Acting Municipal Treasurer undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records.
b. We also recommended that the PPELC maintained by the Accounting Unit and Property Cards maintained by the Property Unit be reconciled and complete all other procedures in the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason:
Currently, the Inventory Committee is still performing a physical count of properties in the various offices of the Municipality.
The reconciliation of records is still on-going. There is also no request as to the one-time cleansing submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make reconciliation on the reciprocal accounts and make adjustments accordingly so the balances reported in the financial statements be more reflective of the actual financial position of the Municipality as of the reporting period.
b. However, if these accounts are found to be inexistent, request for write off following existing COA guidelines.
observations.reason:
This finding is reiterated in the CY 2024 AAR
This finding is reiterated in the CY 2024 AAR

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant promptly deduct the outstanding amount from the monthly salaries of those still in service upon the terms agreed upon by the Municipality and the respective employee.
b. Furthermore, for Accountable Officers and employees who are no longer in service or are already deceased, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant initiate the process for writing off their accounts in accordance with the prevailing regulations.
c. Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.reason:
No deductions as to salaries were made by the Accounting Office as the Cash Advances still exist.
A request for write-off was submitted to the Auditor but there are lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee under the Acting Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts to locate the necessary property records or supporting documents needed to prepare the IIRUP to be used as basis for inspection and appraisal.
b. We also recommended that the Inventory Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the subject unserviceable properties. The Committee shall prepare a plan/timetable of disposal activities to be undertaken and submit a copy to the auditor.
observations.reason:
No disposals made in CY 2024.
No disposals made in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Chairperson of the Inventory Committee, agreed to collaborate to reconcile the ₱7.565 million variance which should include full compliance with the provisions for a one-time cleansing, specifically the complete execution of all reconciliation procedures, to ensure accurate and reliable PPE balances in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that depreciation is calculated and recorded accurately for all eligible PPE within the correct accounting period. Unrecorded depreciation pertaining to CY 2024 should be recorded as a Prior Period Adjustment, thus assuring the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant conformed to record the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the assets in the General Fund and retroactively calculate and record the accumulated depreciation expense from the date the assets were fully paid and completed once proper documentation has been established. Otherwise, these assets shall be included in the One-Time Cleansing of PPE due to unavailability of records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a thorough reconciliation of all intra-agency receivables and payables, specifically the "Due from Other Funds" and "Due to Other Funds" accounts, identify and correct any discrepancies, ensuring that the balances reflect accurate and synchronized transactions.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer conformed to reconcile the RPT and SET Receivable with the Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts, identify discrepancies, and implement the necessary adjustments to affected accounts while properly documenting the process. Henceforth, implement documented regular reviews of the accounts to prevent similar occurrences in the future.
We finally recommended and management assented to enforce a policy requiring the synchronous recording of all reciprocal transactions such as implementing internal controls and regular reconciliation procedures to detect and prevent asynchronous recording.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to develop a clear guide for classifying expenditures, distinguishing between capital expenditures/investments and social welfare or operational expenses, and to refrain from obligating and charging 20% DF for expenditures which are not directly related to the implementation of development projects.

We recommended and the LDRRM Officer conformed to revisit the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and propose projects that are in line with the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction management as set forth under the JMC.
We further recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from obligating and charging LDRRMF for expenditures which are not directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities.

We recommended and management agreed to revisit and restructure their GAD programs to maximize the impact of its GAD initiatives, ensuring that allocated funds are effectively deployed to advance gender equality and women's empowerment in line with national mandates.
Also, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor assented to fully enforce its GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team that will be responsible for overseeing the implementation of the GAD system, as mandated by Section 3.4 of the same circular to strengthen GAD mainstreaming efforts.
Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and GAD Focal Person agreed to perform a regular reconciliation procedure to detect and prevent asynchronous recording of the GAD records.

We recommended and the LSB agreed to furnish the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Treasurer of the SEF Budget within seven days from its approval to ensure timely budget implementation and proper financial management of the SEF.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant conformed to immediately designate a dedicated focal person responsible for the timely submission of all quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the mandated recipients within the prescribed deadlines to ensure strict compliance with DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and to prevent future lapses in transparency and accountability hindering effective oversight of SEF funds.

a. We recommended that the Chairperson of the Inventory Committee under the Acting Municipal Treasurer fast track the processing of titles of the Land and submit complete supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant.
b. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the above-mentioned properties in the books of the Municipality by drawing necessary journal entries in the PPE account.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer verify the bank balances in the depository banks and subsequently perform appropriate action such as returning excess funds to identified NGAs or reverting the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at the end of each quarter. Any discrepancies requiring adjustment should be promptly addressed and resolved to ensure accurate presentation of Cash in Bank balances in the Financial Statements.

a. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort in verifying the amount under “For Verification” in the Due to LGUs account. If found that these cannot be traced anymore, the Municipal Accountant should request for the write-off on the balances of fund transfers recorded in the Due to LGUs account following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
b. Henceforth, we advised that the Municipal Accountant consistently oversee the receipt and utilization of fund transfers from other LGUs. Any remaining funds after implementation should be returned to the Source Agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant validate the list of unserviceable PPEs for disposal from the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Head – Property Unit and undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties.
Moreover, after disposal, the Municipal Accountant must prepare adjusting entries to derecognize or drop said unserviceable items from the books.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in order to present reliable Cash – Local Treasury and Cash in Bank balances.
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer activate the dormant accounts and properly transfer it to the active account of the authorized government depository bank of the Municipality.

1.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
and the Chairperson of the Inventory Committee under the supervision of
the Acting Municipal Treasurer undertake collaborative procedures to
ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their
respective records.
1.9 We also recommended that the PPELC maintained by the Accounting Unit
and Property Cards maintained by the Property Unit be reconciled and
complete all other procedures in the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

2.6 We recommended and Management concurred that the Chairperson of the
Inventory Committee under the Acting Municipal Treasurer fast track the
processing of titles of the Land and submit complete supporting documents
to the Municipal Accountant.
2.7 Henceforth, we recommended and Management affirmed that the
Municipal Accountant include the above-mentioned properties in the books
of the Municipality by drawing necessary journal entries in the PPE
account.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a yearly physical count and submit to COA a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment containing the cost, accountable officer assigned, and actual status of the assets in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officers and employees who are still in services to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents for the liquidation of their cash advances. For those who are still in service but unable to settle their cash advances, it is recommended that the same be deducted from their monthly salaries in the amount agreed upon by the Municipality and the employee.

Moreover, for Accountable Officers and employees who are not anymore in service or those who are deceased, We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the request for write-off of their accounts in consonance with existing regulations.

Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agency once the projects/programs are completed or as agreed in the MOA.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant revert to the General Fund the balances of fund transfers with details that cannot be traced anymore in consonance with COA Circular No. 94-013 so it may be used for other purposes to the advantage of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the donations at the date of receipt and recognize the amount of unused vaccines as Inventory at year-end at their fair value which may be ascertained by reference to an active market or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant facilitate and supervise the processing of the TCTs of lots to establish absolute ownership and protect the City's legal interests in these real properties.

Moreover, an inventory of lots owned by the Municipality shall be included in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment submitted to the Office of the Auditor to properly monitor real properties owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the MPDC ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20 Percent Development are well planned, procurement and implementation ready with itemized appropriation for specific development projects.

We also recommended that the Municipality discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in lump-sum amount and instead resort to line budgeting to ensure regularity and transparency of government transactions. In case lumpsum appropriations cannot be avoided, Management must secure prior authority from the SB before disbursement of funds is authorized.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a yearly physical count, and submit to the Auditor a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment containing the actual status of the assets, after which reconciliation of records shall be made in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for proper disposal and after the sale of these unserviceable properties be dropped from the books.
We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer, the Assistant Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Accountant fast track the corroboration of data of unserviceable PPE accounts under verification and submit a Request for Authority to Derecognize Non-Existing PPEs as required under Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in order to present reliable Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to submit immediately Liquidation Reports and supporting documents for the settlement of cash advances totaling P3,228.283.74.
Moreover, for employees who are still in the service but unable to settle their cash advances, it is recommended that Municipal Accountant deduct from monthly salaries the amount of unliquidated cash advance, otherwise, require them to refund the unsettled balance.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant require that cash advances be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish copies of all contracts, purchase orders and assignment /work orders within five days from issuance, for the Auditor to conduct appropriate action in verifying that actual delivery by the supplier took place pursuant to existing regulations.

We recommended that the NIP Coordinator furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant with a detailed list of allocated vaccines received by the Municipality for the latter to recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations.
For donations received without costs, the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall recognize the donations at fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.
We recommended that the Municipal Government exert effort to promote vaccine confidence in order to encourage more of its populace to be vaccinated in order to attain the herd immunity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast track the implementation of the fund transfers with available records, or if such cannot anymore be implemented, return the unspent balance to the source LGU or NGA.
We further recommended the Municipal Accountant to revert to the General Fund that balance of fund transfers with details that cannot be traced anymore so it may be used for other purposes to the advantage of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and Acting Municipal Treasurer undertake the required reconciliation of property records pursuant to Section 114 of Manual on NGAS, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.
observations.reason: Inventory count still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Appraisal/Disposal Committee to assess the value of these properties and facilitate request to the COA Technical Team which will set the floor price for public auction sale.  We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for proper disposal after which they shall be dropped from the accounts.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering records of unserviceabl e properties

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in order to present reliable Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank balances.
observations.reason: Reconciliati on still on-going between the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant. Various adjustments are still under verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees still connected with the Municipality to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents for the liquidation of cash advances, otherwise require the refund of the amount of cash advance.  Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Accountable officers who are not in service were given demand letters but have not complied yet. Accounting office has difficulty in gathering death certificates of deceased accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the appropriation and expenses for Personal Services are limited to the required fifty five percent (55%) of the total annual income from regular sources realized by the Municipality in the next preceding fiscal year as prescribed by Section 325(a) of R.A. 7160.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the prescribed format of GPPB for submission by HoPE to GPPB and the same be posted for transparency and measurement purposes.

We recommended that the Municipality require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the supplier and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases especially when the documentary requirements are not complete after the contract has been awarded.

We recommended the OIC-MSWDO ensure the preparation of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnish the COA Auditor within ten (10) days after the end of the quarter to enable proper monitoring and evaluation of donations received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and Acting Municipal Treasurer undertake the required reconciliation of property records pursuant to Section 114 of Manual on NGAS, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.
Furthermore, Accounting Office and Acting Municipal Treasurer investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Appraisal/Disposal Committee to assess the value of these properties and facilitate request to the COA Technical Team which will set the floor price for public auction sale.
We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for proper disposal after which they shall be dropped from the accounts.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in order to present reliable Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees still connected with the Municipality to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents for the liquidation of cash advances, otherwise require the refund of the amount of cash advance.
Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the appropriation and expenses for Personal Services are limited to the required fifty five percent (55%) of the total annual income from regular sources realized by the Municipality in the next preceding fiscal year as prescribed by Section 325(a) of R.A. 7160.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the prescribed format of GPPB for submission by HoPE to GPPB and the same be posted for transparency and measurement purposes.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the supplier and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases especially when the documentary requirements are not complete after the contract has been awarded.

We recommended the OIC-MSWDO ensure the preparation of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnish the COA Auditor within ten (10) days after the end of the quarter to enable proper monitoring and evaluation of donations received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality implements the programs, projects and activities under the SWM Program in the 20% development fund which includes the purchase of lot for dumpsite and develop it into a sanitary landfill in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003 or if not possible, develop a new technological approach to solid waste management where wastes are better managed, controlled and contained.
observations.reason: No construction of dumpsite for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  conduct  a  yearly
   physical count and submit a report thereon on or before January 31 of the
   following year in accordance with existing regulations.


        Moreover, the Report on  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and
   Equipment  must  contain  the  actual  status  of  the   assets   whether
   unserviceable, scrap or already junk so that disposal of these properties
   could be undertaken and appropriate adjustments be made to fairly present
   these accounts in the financial statements.


        For properties with no available historical records the same should
   be appraised/revalued, by an Appraisal Committee to  be  created  by  the
   Municipal Mayor so that appropriate actions be undertaken in establishing
   records for proper accounting of PPE.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  thru   the   Municipal
   Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees  concerned  to
   submit immediately Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to settle
   the unliquidated cash advance totaling  P3,160,469.98,  otherwise  refund
   the amount advanced.


        Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed
   period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8  of  COA  Circular
               No. 97-002.

We recommend that Management:


     a. Involve closely the barangay officials in the strict implementation
        of  RA  9003  and  intensification  of  the  “no  segregation,   no
        collection policy” among residents;


     b. Exercise strong political will to implement the environmental  laws
        on preserving and protecting the environment  as  mandated  by  the
        Act.


     c. Fast track the construction of dumpsite and  Eco-Center  in  a  lot
        owned by the municipality wherein  wastes  maybe  processed  in  an
        environmentally sound manner.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order  or
   Administrative Order creating the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation  Team  to
   oversee the implementation of the GAD  plans  and  projects  as  required
   under existing laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality implements all the projects and
   activities embodied in the LDRRM Fund and also give importance  to  pre-
   disaster programs such as trainings that could enhance the capability of
   the officials and personnel of the local government unit to mitigate the
   impact when disaster occurs for the benefit of its constituents.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipality  give  importance  to  the
   implementation of the projects funded by  other  government  agencies  as
   required in the Memorandum of Agreement, so that the  purpose  for  which
   these funds were granted are achieved thus, benefitting its constituents.

We recommended   the Inventory    Committee  to conduct a yearly  physical count   and submit a     report thereon on or before January 31 of the        following year in accordance with Section 490 of the GAAM Volume I, and Section 57 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
Moreover, the RCPPE must contain the actual status of the assets whether unserviceable, scrap or already junk so that disposal of these properties could be undertaken while the properties are still with salvage value.
Properties with no available historical records should be appraised/revalued, by an Appraisal Committee to be created by the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to complete the count as well as absence of records of property, plant and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly to present reliable balances of Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliati on of records is still on-going and adjustments are still being verified by both offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the start of the year, a duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due for the year for the booking up of the RPT/SET Receivables as provided in Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I so that the actual realizable RPT Income will be reflected in the financial reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees concerned to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to settle the cash advances totaling P4,387,804.38.
Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers are not connected with the LGU anymore and some are deceased. For those which are still in service, demand letters have been sent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2013 of the DILG and DBM.

We recommended that the Municipality implements all the projects and activities embodied in the LDRRM Fund and also give importance to pre-disaster programs such as trainings that could enhance the capability of the officials and personnel of the local government unit to mitigate the impact when disaster occurs for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.reason: Programs were mainly focused on labor. Pre-disaster programs such as strengthenin g of DRRM and emergency response were not prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality give importance to the implementation of the projects funded by other government agencies as required in the Memorandum of Agreement so that the purpose for which these funds were granted are achieved thus, benefitting its constituents.
observations.reason: Projects are for bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant booked up RPT/SET Receivables based on RPTAR/TIC at the start of the year as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I so that the actual realizable RPT Income will be reflected in the financial reports. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office intensify a massive campaign in order to effect an improve collection on RPT so that more development programs may be implemented for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.reason: The  DIC Municipal Treasurer did not furnish copy of RPTAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management compute and deduct from the claim of the contractor a liquidated damages for the 1,356 days of delay in the completion of the project in compliance with Items 8.1 and 8.5 of RA 9184. We recommended further for management to submit the approved suspension of work order and resume to work order/extension of contract time to support the delay in the completion of the project, if any.
observations.reason: The delay of the work/project was due to the delay of the downloading of funds and not at the fault of the contractors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Inventory Committee to conduct a yearly physical
   count and submit a  report  thereon  on  or  before  January  31  of  the
   following year in accordance with Section 490 of the GAAM Volume  I,  and
   Section 57 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. II.


        Moreover, the RCPPE must contain the actual status  of  the  assets
   whether unserviceable, scrap or already junk so that  disposal  of  these
   properties could be  undertaken  while  the  properties  are  still  with
   salvage value.


        Properties  with  no  available  historical   records   should   be
   appraised/revalued, by an  Appraisal  Committee  to  be  created  by  the
   Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  the  Municipal
   Accountant reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and
   any items for adjustment must  be  corrected  and  resolved  promptly  to
   present reliable balances of Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts.

We  recommended  that  the  OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  furnish   the
   Municipal Accountant at the start of the year, a duly certified  list  of
   taxpayers with the amount due for the year for  the  booking  up  of  the
   RPT/SET Receivables as provided in Section 20 of the Manual on  the  NGAS
   for LGUs, Volume I so that the  actual  realizable  RPT  Income  will  be
   reflected in the financial reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  and  Municipal  Accountant
   require the Accountable  Officials  and  employees  concerned  to  submit
   immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to  settle
   the cash advances totaling P4,387,804.38.


        Henceforth,  cash  advances  should  be   liquidated   within   the
   prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of  COA
   Circular               No. 97-002.

We recommended  that  the  Municipality  implements  the  programs,
   projects and activities under the SWM Program in the 20% development fund
   which includes the purchase of lot for dumpsite and  develop  it  into  a
   sanitary landfill in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003 or if  not
   possible, develop a new technological approach to solid waste  management
   where wastes are better managed, controlled and contained.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the conduct of a yearly physical count and submit a report thereon on or before January 31 of the following year in accordance with Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I, and Section 57 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. II. Moreover, the RPCPPE must contain the actual status of the assets whether unserviceable, scrap or already junk so disposal of these properties could be undertaken while the properties are still with salvage value. Properties with no available historical records should be appraised/revalued by an Appraisal Committee to be created by the Municipal Mayor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created the inventory committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office manually compute for the depreciation of the properties mentioned above. Also, depreciation should be provided monthly in the future periods.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adheres strictly to the provisions of the NGAS Manual in the timely submission of the financial reports.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should effect adjustment in the GF so as to reflect the correct balance per book as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees concerned to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to settle the unliquidated cash advance totaling P4,219,507.40. Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Some of the previous cash advances were liquidated. Only employees who were separated resigned or dead are with outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry voucher to take up the payment made on the loan with the LBP-Bacolod and the corresponding expense accounts to reflect the correct balances as at year-end. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a quarterly reconciliation of loan accounts with creditor-banks to ensure early detection and resolution.

Recommendations: Implement the environmental laws to preserve and protect the environment as mandated by the Act; Involve closely the barangay officials in the strict implementation of RA 9003 and intensification of the “no segregation, no collection policy”; Direct the SWMB to ensure that all barangays in the city have Material Recovery Facilities; Negotiate the purchase of the lot and consider the closure of the open dump site as mandated under Section 37 of the Act and the possibility of acquiring a sanitary landfill and/or converting a new technological approach to solid waste management where wastes are better managed, controlled and contained.
observations.reason: Barangay officials were involve in the “ no segregation , no collection policy ‘ and the LGU is about to purchase a lot for the construction of MRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor give importance on the implementation of the projects funded by other government agencies as required in the Memorandum of Agreement so that the purpose for which these funds were granted are achieved thus, benefitting its constituents.
observations.reason: Some projects are implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit acknowledgement receipts of the recipients for the 52 sacks rice as basis for confirmation in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor endeavor to maximize the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2013 of the DILG and DBM.

Recommendations:

        We recommended the Municipal Mayor to  require  the  conduct  of  a
   yearly physical count and submit a report thereon on or before January 31
   of the following year in accordance with Section 490 of GAAM,  Volume  I,
   and Section 57 of the Manual on  NGAS for Local  Government  Units,  Vol.
   II.


        Moreover, the RPCPPE must contain the actual status of  the  assets
   whether unserviceable,  scrap  or  already  junk  so  disposal  of  these
   properties could be  undertaken  while  the  properties  are  still  with
   salvage value.


        Properties  with  no  available  historical   records   should   be
   appraised/revalued by  an  Appraisal  Committee  to  be  created  by  the
   Municipal Mayor.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the Accounting Office manually compute for the
   depreciation of the properties mentioned above. Also, depreciation should
   be provided monthly in the future periods.

Recommendation

        We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  adheres  strictly  to
   the provisions of the  NGAS  Manual  in  the  timely  submission  of  the
   financial reports.

Recommendation

        We  recommended  that  Municipal  Mayor  instruct   the   Municipal
   Accountant  to  prepare  and  submit  the  monthly  Bank   Reconciliation
   Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 to ascertain the  accuracy
   and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank  account.   Likewise,  the  Municipal
   Accountant should effect adjustment in  the  GF  so  as  to  reflect  the
   correct balance per book as of December 31, 2017.

Recommendations:

        We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and  the  Municipal
   Accountant booked up RPT/SET Receivables based on RPTAR/TIC at the  start
   of the year as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on  the  NGAS  for
   LGUs, Volume I so that the actual realizable RPT Income will be reflected
   in the financial reports.


        We  further  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer’s  Office
   intensify a massive campaign in order to effect an improve collection  on
   RPT so that more development programs may be implemented for the  benefit
   of its constituents.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  and  Municipal  Accountant
   require the Accountable  Officials  and  employees  concerned  to  submit
   immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to  settle
   the unliquidated cash advance totaling P4,219,507.40.


        Henceforth,  cash  advances  should  be   liquidated   within   the
   prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of  COA
   Circular                  No. 97-002.

Recommendation

        We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  a  journal
   entry voucher to take up the payment made  on  the  loan  with  the  LBP-
   Bacolod and the corresponding expense accounts  to  reflect  the  correct
   balances as at year-end.


        We further recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  a
   quarterly reconciliation of loan accounts with creditor-banks  to  ensure
   early detection and resolution.

Recommendations:

   a.  Implement  the  environmental  laws  to  preserve  and  protect   the
      environment as mandated by the Act;

   b. Involve closely the barangay officials in the strict implementation of
      RA 9003 and intensification of  the  “no  segregation,  no  collection
      policy” among residents;

   c. Direct the SWMB to ensure that all barangays in the city have Material
      Recovery Facilities; and

   d. Negotiate the purchase of the lot and consider the closure of the open
      dump site as mandated under Section 37 of the Act and the  possibility
      of acquiring a sanitary landfill and/or converting a new technological
      approach to solid waste management where wastes  are  better  managed,
      controlled and contained.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  give  importance  on  the
   implementation of the projects funded by  other  government  agencies  as
   required in the Memorandum of Agreement so that  the  purpose  for  which
   these funds were granted are achieved thus, benefitting its constituents.

Recommendation

        We recommended that management submit acknowledgement  receipts  of
   the recipients for the  52  sacks  rice  as  basis  for  confirmation  in
   consonance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

Recommendations:

        We recommended that management compute and deduct from the claim of
   the contractor a liquidated damages for the 1,356 days of  delay  in  the
   completion of the project in compliance with Items  8.1  and  8.5  of  RA
   9184.

         We recommended further  for  management  to  submit  the  approved
   suspension of work order and resume to work order/extension  of  contract
   time  to  support the delay in the completion of the project, if any.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a yearly physical count and submit a report thereon on or before January 31 of the following year in accordance with Section 490 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I, and Section 57 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Vol. II. Moreover, the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment must contain the actual status of the assets whether unserviceable, scrap or already junk so that disposal of these properties could be undertaken while the properties are still with salvage value. Properties with no available historical records should be appraised/revalued, by an Appraisal Committee to be created by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommend that the Accounting Office record the loss as “Prior Period Adjustments” and charge it against the appropriate receivable account of Ms. Mary Rose Juain, Ms. Sarah B. Agui and Ms. Marie Grace Marmito in the amount of P41,640.00, P27,145.16 and P32,725.50, respectively pending the decision on the application for relief of accountability.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant require the Accountable Officials and employees concerned to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to settle the unliquidated cash advance totaling P5,266,080.59.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the Accountable Officials and employees concerned and upon failure to liquidate the cash advance, deduct from their salary the amount of cash advanced especially those pertaining to traveling expenses.

Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to
conduct a yearly physical count and submit a report  thereon  on  or  before
January 31 of the following year in  accordance  with  Section  490  of  the
Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I,  and  Section  57
of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for  Local  Government
Units, Vol. II.


      Moreover,  the  Report  on  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and
Equipment  must  contain  the  actual   status   of   the   assets   whether
unserviceable, scrap or already junk so that disposal  of  these  properties
could be undertaken while the properties are still with salvage value.

      Properties  with   no   available   historical   records   should   be
appraised/revalued,  by  an  Appraisal  Committee  to  be  created  by   the
Municipal Mayor.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  should  provide  for  the
depreciation of its property, plant and  equipment.  Adjustments  should  be
recorded  as  “Prior  Years’  Adjustment”  in  the  subsequent  year.  Also,
depreciation should be provided monthly in the future periods.


      The  Municipal  Accountant  assured  us  that  depreciation  will   be
monitored in the subsequent years and necessary adjustments will be made.

We recommend  that  the  BAC  Secretariat  strictly  comply  with  the
provision of RA No. 9184 and provide  the  Office  of  the  Auditor  with  a
screenshot of the Notice of Award posted in the PhilGEPS page.

We recommend that the Accounting Office  record  the  loss  as  “Prior
Period  Adjustments”  and  charge  it  against  the  appropriate  receivable
account of Ms. Mary Rose Juain, Ms.  Sarah  B.  Agui  and  Ms.  Marie  Grace
Marmito  in  the  amount   of   P41,640.00,   P27,145.16   and   P32,725.50,
respectively  pending  the  decision  on  the  application  for  relief   of
accountability.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  and  Municipal  Accountant
require the Accountable  Officials  and  employees  concerned  to  submit
immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to  settle
the unliquidated cash advance totaling P5,266,080.59.


        We further recommend that the  Municipal  Accountant  issue  demand
letters to the Accountable Officials and  employees  concerned  and  upon
failure to liquidate the cash  advance,  deduct  from  their  salary  the
amount  of  cash  advanced  especially  those  pertaining  to   traveling
expenses.

        Henceforth,  cash  advances  should  be   liquidated   within   the
prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Section 5.8 of  COA
Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a yearly physical count and submit a report thereon on or before January 31 of the following year in accordance with Section 490 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I, and Section 57 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Vol. II.
Moreover, the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment must contain the actual status of the assets whether unserviceable, scrap or already junk so disposal of these properties could be undertaken while the properties are still with salvage value.
Properties with no available historical records should be appraised/revalued, by an Appraisal Committee to be created by the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers and paid payrolls immediately to the Auditor’s Office and all subsequent transactions within 10 days of each month after it has been recorded in the books of accounts pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant submits all overdue financial reports. Henceforth, she must adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 70 & 73 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume I and Section 122 of Presidential Decree 1445.

The Municipal Mayor imposes sanctions stated in Section 122 of Presidential Decree 1445 if non-submission becomes a constant practice by person’s responsible.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant promptly submit the monthly bank reconciliation statement to the auditor for proper verification in order to ascertain the correctness of the balances at the end of the period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to pay its obligations to the contractor in the amount of P404,203.64, remit to BIR the contractor’s tax of P162,174.29 and release the remaining LGU counterpart equity for the ALBAPA Farm to Market Road project so the contractor could immediately resume its work.

Moreover, he should immediately submit the audited financial reports required by the Department of Agrarian Reform so the final tranche of loan proceeds will finally be released.
